summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a historic show highlighted by an explosive Blues For Allah jam and blistering Johnny B. Goode.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced soundboard source that most listeners describe as “as good as you’re gonna get,” with only occasional minor cuts noted. Musically the concert is praised for its intense, focused jamming – especially the half‑hour Blues For Allah improvisation and a fiery Johnny B. Goode – and many reviewers call it a rare gem that hinted at the band’s future direction.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (9 out of 12) are 4‑5 stars and specifically commend both performance and sound quality; only a few low‑star comments focus on band‑member preferences rather than the recording itself.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with excellent balance; a couple of reviewers note minor repeats and occasional cuts but overall regarded as top‑tier for the era.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as a tight, focused half‑hour Blues For Allah jam that serves as the centerpiece, bookended by high‑energy numbers like Johnny B. Goode; reviewers feel the performance flows brilliantly and foreshadows the band’s later funk‑fusion direction.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note that Merl Saunders’ keyboards push Jerry’s guitar playing to new heights, calling his tone “perfect” and the interplay “jazzy”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Both Merl Saunders and Keith Godchaux receive high praise; Saunders is hailed for his improvisational fire while Keith’s piano work is defended as some of the best in Dead history.
